en.extract:
The citrine warbler (Myiothlypis luteoviridis) is a species of bird in the family Parulidae. The term citrine refers to its yellowish coloration. It is found in Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Venezuela.  Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.


Behaviour
Pairs of citrine warblers are known to forage in the lower levels and edges of forests. They often do this among mixed-species flocks.

The '''citrine warbler''' ('''''Myiothlypis luteoviridis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Parulidae]]. The term [[Citrine (colour)|''citrine'']] refers to its yellowish coloration. It is found in [[Bolivia]], [[Colombia]], [[Ecuador]], [[Peru]], and [[Venezuela]].  Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.<ref name="BeadleQuinn2010"/>

==Behaviour==
Pairs of citrine warblers are known to forage in the lower levels and edges of forests. They often do this among mixed-species flocks.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Citrine Warbler - eBird|url=https://ebird.org/species/citwar1|access-date=2021-07-06|website=ebird.org|language=en}}</ref>
